text:
Recent advancements in Vision and Language (VL) models, as well as Large Language Models (LLMs), have demonstrated superior performance across a variety of benchmark evaluation sets and tasks. However, current state-of-the-art VL and LLMs are lacking in the context of video-centric understanding that leverages the benefits of both vision and audition. In this paper, we present Video-LLaMA that aims to alleviate this void. The proposed multi-modal framework makes Video-LLaMA suitable for learning from video-based data via instruction-tuning, which inherently obtains cross-modal alignment from frozen, pre-trained visual and audio encoders. Current VL and LLMs are still lacking in several crucial aspects when it comes to video-based understanding tasks. For instance, most models solely rely on vision alone and hence, they fall short of effectively representing the temporal information in the visual information present in videos, and most models treat the audio streams in a sub-word level manner rather than jointly using audio-visual information that could greatly improve the output of models.

So far, we discussed how to model the spatial and contextual aspects. Now, we will discuss how to model the temporal aspect of visual understanding, and how Audio-LLaMA perceives the auditory information in speech. Video Q-former In Video-LLaMA, we adopt a pre-trained image encoder for the Video Q-former by adapting it to video, and learn the Video-LLaMA model by completing a video-to-text generation task to train the video-language correspondence. Audio Q-former We utilize the ImageBind as a universal backbone for Audio-LLaMA, and propose Audio Q-former that is used to encode the complex speech audio into the embedding space of LLM. Our Audio-LLaMA model is trained in a multi-stage fashion: we first train Audio-LLaMA model with large-scale alignment on a large video-captioning dataset, and then fine-tune it on a high-quality visual instruction dataset. We show that Video-LLaMA can achieve state-of-the-art results on several benchmark datasets, and it is capable of understanding the intricate details in videos and produces accurate and context-aware answers based on what it “sees” and “hears”.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written

Current VL and LLMs are still lacking in several crucial aspects when it comes to video-based understanding tasks. For instance, most models solely rely on vision alone and hence, they fall short of effectively representing the temporal information in the visual information present in videos, and most models treat the audio streams in a sub-word level manner rather than jointly using audio-visual information that could greatly improve the output of models.

So far, we discussed how to model the spatial and contextual aspects.
